description: A supporting structure is 'kelo' when it surrounds an opening or gives an object its underlying shape. Window frames, picture frames, and load-carrying frames all qualify. 'norui' (boundary) names a limit whether or not anything material stands there; 'kelo' is the structure itself.
articulatory_notes: Form 'k' by closing the back of the tongue against the soft palate, then release into stressed mid-front 'e'. Set the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for 'l' and round the lips into mid-back 'o'.

examples.translation: I make a frame for the window.

semantic_domains.creation: a supporting structure made around or within another object
semantic_domains.physical: the material support that holds an opening or overall shape
semantic_domains.spatial: a structure defining the extent of an opening
